div.h3: SPSC Ring Buffer: Step-by-step memory operations with cache line analysis

div.p: Total cross-core traffic: 1-2 cache line transfers

ul.li: 🟩 Cache Hit - Line in local L1
🟪 Fence - CPU barrier, no memory access

div.p: Cache Outcome: ○ May be cache MISS on first access ○ Consumer's cache line may be in Modified state ○ Inter-core traffic if consumer recently wrote
No Memory Access: ✓ All values in registers ✓ head was loaded in previous iteration ✓ mask is constant (header field) ✓ Zero cache line touches
Decision: If full → return false If space → continue to step 3

Slot Memory Layout: ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Slot[pos & mask] │ │ ├─ Byte 0x00: Message data... │ │ ├─ Byte 0x01: ... │ │ ├─ ... │ │ └─ Byte N: (up to slot_size) │ │ │ │ Write pattern: sequential stores │ │ May span multiple cache lines │ └────────────────────────────────────────┘

Cache Behavior: ○ First write: cache MISS (RFO - Read For Ownership) ○ Subsequent writes: cache HIT (same line) ○ Producer's L1: line in Modified state ○ No cross-core traffic (producer owns this line)
Memory Fence (Release): Ensures ALL prior memory operations complete BEFORE any subsequent memory operations. Specifically: memcpy stores become visible BEFORE head update becomes visible.
CPU Barrier Instruction: x86/x64: mfence or implicit (strong ordering) ARM64: dmb ish (Data Memory Barrier) POWER: lwsync (Lightweight Sync) No memory access - CPU pipeline stall only

Establishes Ordering: ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ memcpy(slot, data, size) │ │ ↓ │ │ ═════ RELEASE FENCE ═════ │ │ ↓ │ │ store(head, next) ← visible AFTER │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘
Cache Outcome: ○ Cache HIT (producer owns this line) ○ Line stays in producer's L1 (hot) ○ Consumer will see cache MISS when reading ○ No immediate cross-core traffic
